_schema: RAGAS v0.3 SingleTurnSample compatible (user_input, reference, reference_contexts) plus eval metadata (persona, expected_query_type, difficulty, expected_anchor_entities).
_methodology: Hand-curated using real anchors mined from entities.sqlite3 (8.02M entities) and source_path patterns from the enterprise program corpus Tier 1 extraction. No LLM-generated queries, no fictitious identifiers.
_note: Phase 1 delivers 50 new queries (PQ-101 through PQ-150). The existing 25 queries at tests/golden_eval/production_queries_2026-04-11.json (PQ-001 through PQ-025) will be merged into this file in a future pass; do not double-count. Target for full Phase 2+ is 400 total.

user_input: What is the latest enterprise program weekly hours variance for fiscal year 2024?
expected_source_patterns: 10.0 Program Management/2.0 Weekly Variance Reports/2024/%enterprise program Weekly Hours Variance%
rationale: Tests the PM's primary cost/schedule pulse check: direct spreadsheet lookup against a recurring standardized artifact. Real weekly variance reports exist for every Friday in 2024.
corpus_grounding_evidence: Entity store contains 15,256 entity hits on 2024-12-31 Weekly Hours Variance.xlsx alone; the artifact family has dozens of dated siblings under 2.0 Weekly Variance Reports/2024/.

user_input: What are the FEP monthly actuals for 2024 and how do they roll up across months?
rationale: Aggregation across 12 monthly actuals files. Tests whether retrieval can enumerate the full 2024 FEP series without missing months.
corpus_grounding_evidence: Folder listing confirmed via anchor miner: '1.0 FEP/Reference/2024 NN Monthly Actuals.xlsx' exists for each month with 7,886-7,930 entity hits per file.

user_input: Which CDRL is A002 and what maintenance service reports have been submitted under it?
reference: A002 is the Maintenance Service Report (MSR) CDRL. Submissions are organized by site under 1.5 enterprise program CDRLS/A002 - Maintenance Service Report (MSR)/ with 21 site-specific subfolders covering Alpena through Wake.
reference_contexts: 1.5 enterprise program CDRLS/A002 - Maintenance Service Report (MSR)/
expected_source_patterns: 1.5 enterprise program CDRLS/A002 - Maintenance Service Report (MSR)/%
rationale: CDRL code -> deliverable type lookup. Tests that exact identifier 'A002' matches against the folder naming convention.
corpus_grounding_evidence: 21 MSR site subfolders confirmed via corpus folder listing; A002 is the canonical CDRL code for Maintenance Service Reports per the folder naming.

user_input: What is the enterprise program Integrated Master Schedule deliverable and where is it tracked?
expected_source_patterns: 1.5 enterprise program CDRLS/A031 - Integrated Master Schedule (IMS)/%
rationale: Tests narrative recall of CDRL structure. A031 is the IMS deliverable folder.
corpus_grounding_evidence: A031 IMS folder exists in the canonical enterprise program CDRLS folder tree per earlier corpus exploration.

user_input: What is the full set of CDRL deliverables for the enterprise program?
reference: The enterprise program contract defines 20+ CDRL deliverables under 1.5 enterprise program CDRLS/. Major ones include: A001 Corrective Action Plan, A002 Maintenance Service Report, A003 Site Installation Plan, A004 DD250 Transfer, A006 Installation Acceptance Test Plan, A007 Installation Acceptance Test Report, A008 Program Management Plan, A009 Monthly Status Report, A010 Maintenance Support Plan (SSP), A011 Configuration Audit Report, A012 Configuration Management Plan, A013 System Engineering Management Plan (SEMP), A014 Priced Bill of Materials, A023 Integrated Logistics Support Plan, A027 DAA Accreditation Support Data (ACAS, SCAP, POA&M, RMF Security Plan, etc.), A031 Integrated Master Schedule, A050 Configuration Change Request, A055 Government Property Inventory.
rationale: Full enumeration of CDRL folder tree. Tests whether retrieval can surface the complete deliverable catalog.
corpus_grounding_evidence: CDRL folder tree contains 20+ numbered folders confirmed via direct corpus exploration; A027 alone has 9+ subfolders for different RMF artifact types.
